title: Expressing opposition to Central Business District Tolling Program of New York City.
summary:
OVERVIEW: This bill expresses Congress’s disapproval of a plan by New York City to charge drivers a fee to enter parts of Manhattan. It aims to block the implementation of this congestion pricing program.

WHO IS AFFECTED:
- Drivers who travel into and out of Manhattan's central business district.
- Residents and businesses in or near the affected area. 

WHAT CHANGES:
- The bill, if enacted, could halt New York City’s tolling plan.
- It signals Congressional opposition to similar programs elsewhere.

WHAT'S DEBATED:
- Supporters say: This protects commuters and businesses from unfair financial burdens.
- Critics say: It interferes with local decisions about transportation funding. 

STATUS: The bill has been introduced in the House of Representatives and referred to committee for consideration.
status: Referred to the House Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure.
